title: USFDA Approves Shionogi's Xocova for Post-Exposure Prevention of Symptomatic COVID-19
summary: Tokyo: Japan's Shionogi & Co said on Monday the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (USFDA) has approved its oral antiviral Ensitrelvir, commercially ‌known as Xocova, ⁠for ⁠the preventive treatment of...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
Tokyo: Japan's Shionogi & Co said on Monday the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (USFDA) has approved its oral antiviral Ensitrelvir, commercially ‌known as Xocova, ⁠for ⁠the preventive treatment of individuals who have been exposed to COVID-19.

"XOCOVA ​is the first and only oral option clinically ​proven to help prevent ⁠symptomatic COVID-19 ‌after exposure ​among ​study participants regardless of ⁠vaccination status," Nathan McCutcheon, CEO of Shionogi's ​U.S. unit, said in ​a statement.

The financial impact of the FDA approval of Xocova in the current fiscal year ‌ending March 2027 has already been reflected in the earnings forecast ⁠announced last month, Shionogi said.

Source : Reuters

Sheeba Farhat Joined Medical Dialogues in 2018 to report on the latest Education news. A Graduate of the University of Delhi, she specializes in covering stories related to Medical Education updates. For inquiries or further information, you can reach her at editorial@medicaldialogues.in.
